title: Modern Layouts: Flexbox & Grid
content:
## 🏗️ Modern CSS Layouts: Flexbox & Grid

Before Flexbox and Grid, achieving complex layouts required CSS hacks and float workarounds. Today, these two layout systems give you precise, intentional control over how elements are positioned on a page.

---

## 1️⃣ Flexbox: One-Dimensional Layout

**Flexbox** excels at distributing elements in a single direction — either a row or a column.

### Enabling Flexbox

```css
.container {
  display: flex; /* Makes children into flex items */
}
```

### The Main Axis vs. Cross Axis

- **Main Axis**: The primary direction (horizontal by default for `row`, vertical for `column`).
- **Cross Axis**: Perpendicular to the main axis.

```css
.container {
  flex-direction: row;     /* Default: left to right */
  flex-direction: column;  /* Stack top to bottom (main axis is now vertical) */
}
```

---

## 2️⃣ Flexbox Alignment

```css
/* Align along MAIN axis (horizontal by default) */
justify-content: flex-start;    /* Pack at start */
justify-content: flex-end;      /* Pack at end */
justify-content: center;        /* Center */
justify-content: space-between; /* Spread: first at start, last at end */
justify-content: space-around;  /* Equal space around each item */
justify-content: space-evenly;  /* Equal space between all (including edges) */

/* Align along CROSS axis (vertical by default) */
align-items: stretch;       /* Default: fill cross axis */
align-items: center;        /* Center on cross axis */
align-items: flex-start;    /* Align to start of cross axis */
align-items: flex-end;      /* Align to end of cross axis */
```

**Perfect centering** (the most requested CSS trick):
```css
.container {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: center; /* Horizontal center */
  align-items: center;     /* Vertical center */
  min-height: 100vh;       /* Full viewport height */
}
```

---

## 3️⃣ Flex Item Properties

```css
.item {
  flex-grow: 1;    /* How much it grows to fill space (default: 0) */
  flex-shrink: 1;  /* How much it shrinks if needed (default: 1) */
  flex-basis: 200px; /* Initial size before grow/shrink */
  flex: 1;           /* Shorthand: grow=1, shrink=1, basis=0 */
  align-self: center; /* Override parent's align-items for this item */
  order: 2;          /* Change visual order without changing HTML */
}
```

`flex-wrap: wrap` allows items to flow onto the next line when they run out of space (default is `nowrap` — squish everything on one line).

---

## 4️⃣ CSS Grid: Two-Dimensional Layout

**CSS Grid** controls both rows AND columns simultaneously. Perfect for page-level layouts.

```css
.grid {
  display: grid;

  /* Define 3 equal columns */
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr;         /* Or: repeat(3, 1fr) */

  /* Define explicit rows */
  grid-template-rows: 80px auto 60px;

  /* Gap between rows and columns */
  gap: 20px;                /* Or: row-gap + column-gap */
}
```

---

## 5️⃣ The `fr` Unit

`fr` stands for **fraction of available space**. It's Grid's most powerful unit:

```css
grid-template-columns: 1fr 2fr 1fr;
/* 3 columns: left takes 25%, middle takes 50%, right takes 25% */
```

---

## 6️⃣ Placing Items in Grid

```css
.item {
  /* Span across columns */
  grid-column: 1 / 3;       /* From column line 1 to line 3 (2 columns wide) */
  grid-column: span 2;       /* Span 2 columns from current position */

  /* Span across rows */
  grid-row: 1 / 3;           /* From row line 1 to line 3 */

  /* Shorthand for both */
  grid-area: 1 / 1 / 3 / 3; /* row-start / col-start / row-end / col-end */
}
```

---

## 7️⃣ Named Grid Areas

A visual, intuitive way to define layout:

```css
.grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 200px 1fr;
  grid-template-rows: 80px 1fr 60px;
  grid-template-areas:
    "header header"
    "sidebar main"
    "footer footer";
}

.header  { grid-area: header; }
.sidebar { grid-area: sidebar; }
.main    { grid-area: main; }
.footer  { grid-area: footer; }
```

---

## 8️⃣ `repeat()` and `minmax()`

```css
/* 12 equal columns */
grid-template-columns: repeat(12, 1fr);

/* Auto-fill as many columns as fit, each min 200px, max 1fr */
grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(200px, 1fr));
```

`minmax(200px, 1fr)` + `auto-fill` = responsive grid with NO media queries!

---

## 9️⃣ When to Use Flexbox vs Grid

| Scenario | Best Tool |
|----------|----------|
| Navbar (row of links) | Flexbox |
| Card group (align items in a row) | Flexbox |
| Page structure (header/sidebar/main/footer) | Grid |
| Two-dimensional layout | Grid |
| Centering a single item | Flexbox |
| Items of different sizes in a grid | Grid |

The best usage is often both: **Grid for the page layout, Flexbox for the components within.**

---

## 🔟 Auto Layout Summary

```css
/* Default: all items in one column */
display: grid;

/* Implicit tracks auto-created */
grid-auto-rows: 150px;

/* Items stacked in one row */
display: grid;
grid-auto-flow: column;
```

---

## 💡 Cheat Sheet

| Property | Layout System | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `display: flex` | Flexbox | Activate |
| `justify-content` | Flexbox | Main axis alignment |
| `align-items` | Flexbox | Cross axis alignment |
| `flex-grow` | Flexbox | Item grows to fill space |
| `flex-wrap` | Flexbox | Allow wrapping to next line |
| `display: grid` | Grid | Activate |
| `grid-template-columns` | Grid | Define columns |
| `gap` | Grid/Flexbox | Space between items |
| `grid-column: span N` | Grid | Item spans multiple columns |
| `minmax()` | Grid | Responsive without media queries |
| `fr` | Grid | Fraction of available space |

questions.question_text: Which property is used to turn an element into a Flex Container?
questions.explanation: Setting 'display: flex' enables the Flexbox model on equal child elements.

questions.question_text: In Flexbox, which property aligns items along the MAIN axis?
questions.explanation: By default, the main axis is horizontal (row).

questions.question_text: In Flexbox, which property aligns items along the CROSS axis?
questions.explanation: Align-items controls centering or stretching along the secondary axis.

questions.question_text: How do you change the main axis from horizontal to vertical in Flexbox?
questions.explanation: Using 'column' swaps the main and cross axes.

options: Allows a flex item to grow and take up remaining space
Determines the width of the border
questions.explanation: Higher values of flex-grow make items take up more space relative to their siblings.

questions.question_text: Which CSS Grid property is used to define the number of columns?
questions.explanation: You can define sizes like 'repeat(3, 1fr)' for three equal columns.

questions.question_text: What is the 'fr' unit in CSS Grid?
questions.explanation: The 'fr' unit is flexible and splits remaining space into parts.

questions.question_text: How do you add space between rows and columns in CSS Grid?
questions.explanation: The 'gap' (or 'grid-gap') property handles gutters between grid items.

questions.question_text: In a 3-column grid, how do you make an item span across 2 columns?
questions.explanation: You can also use 'grid-column: 1 / 3' to specify start and end lines.

questions.question_text: What is the main difference between Flexbox and Grid?
options: Flexbox is 1D (rows OR columns); Grid is 2D (rows AND columns)
Flexbox is only for mobile; Grid is for desktop
Flexbox is older and should not be used
questions.explanation: Use Flexbox for alignment in a single direction; use Grid for complex page layouts.

questions.question_text: Which property allows flex items to wrap onto multiple lines?
questions.explanation: By default, flex items try to fit onto one line (nowrap).

options: Distributes items evenly, placing the first item at the start and the last at the end
Puts a gap of 10px between everyone
questions.explanation: This is a common way to create headers with a logo on the left and nav on the right.

questions.question_text: How do you center an item both vertically and horizontally using Flexbox on the container?
questions.explanation: This 2-line combination is the modern standard for perfect centering.

questions.question_text: Which grid property is a shortcut for 'grid-row' and 'grid-column'?
questions.explanation: Grid-area can also be used with 'grid-template-areas' for logical naming.

questions.question_text: What happens if you set 'display: grid' but don't define any template columns?
options: All items are stacked in one column by default
All items are in one row
questions.explanation: Grid initializes with a single-column explicit grid by default.
